reasoning: The FRED tool estimates a baseline probability of ~0.30 for the rate to exceed the current 0.4% threshold. Given the steady easing of macroeconomic interest rates by the Fed throughout recent months, the savings rate has mostly stayed flat at 0.40% after dipping to 0.38% earlier in the year. The tendency for deposit rates to lag Fed rate cuts or stay flat favors a 'No' resolution. For the December 9, 2025 resolution date, the probability is set lower to 0.25 because savings rates change slowly and there is limited time for a new increase above 0.40% to be released. For the February 7, 2026 resolution date, there is slightly more time for upside volatility or an unexpected delayed adjustment factor to occur, so uncertainty is higher, leading to an estimate of 0.35 (closer to 0.50).
